scenario_title: Idempotent replay of delete_rrset on already-absent data
test_rationale: This test isolates the gray area around replaying a syntactically valid delete_rrset for an RRset that is already absent. The UPDATE touches a name inside the zone and does not rely on prerequisites, so the only meaningful choice is how the server classifies absence. The follow-up QUERY ensures the post-state is the same regardless of whether the server replies NOERROR or NXRRSET, making the divergence reply-only and directly attributable to the protocol underspecification.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rrset on an already-absent RRset as an idempotent no-op.

group_2.behavior: Signals that the targeted RRset did not exist for deletion.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.3 defines delete_rrset semantics when matching data exist, but it does not state whether deleting an already-absent RRset must succeed as an idempotent no-op or fail with NXRRSET. As a result, compliant servers may diverge only in the UPDATE reply code while leaving the zone unchanged.
